name: Alana
subject: 9 photos from the RV Tour
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
My name is Alana, and I'm Joe's digital director. We got back on Monday from our statewide pre-primary tour. After five days, 32 stops, and more than 1,000 miles, I can tell you firsthand that Hoosier |
---

| | Hey Peter,

My name is Alana, and I'm Joe's digital director. We got back on Monday from our statewide pre-primary tour. After five days, 32 stops, and more than 1,000 miles, I can tell you firsthand that Hoosiers are fired up about Joe and his re-election because I was the one taking photos!

Joe always says that he's Hoosiers' hired help in the Senate, and the truth of that is clearer than ever when he's shaking every hand in every room or listening to Hoosiers' concerns about what keeps them up at night and how government can work better for them.

These tours are so important because there's much more wisdom here in Indiana than in Washington. Days on the road give Joe the chance to listen to Hoosiers and bring their common sense back to work with him in Washington.

But they're only possible with your support. **I hope you 'll take a look at some of the photos from our trip and [pitch in today to help keep us out in the RV.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972db/814495408/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180509.2&amount=10")**

We kicked off the tour with a packed room in Fort Wayne. (Amazing crowd for noon on a Thursday!) | [](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972db/814495408/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180509.2&amount=10")
---
Then, we headed to Delaware County. It was especially inspiring to see so many students show up! This group below is from Burris Laboratory School.
[](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972db/814495408/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180509.2&amount=10")
---
Had to stop for a shake in Anderson at Good's Candy Shop.
[](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972db/814495408/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180509.2&amount=10")
---
We launched Farmers for Joe! What a crowd! It was clear that Hoosier farmers still want a senator who works as hard as they do.
[](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972db/814495408/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180509.2&amount=10")
---
Sullivan was fired up when we rolled in. They had music playing on full blast and some were so excited to see Joe, they ran up and gave him a hug.
[](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972db/814495408/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180509.2&amount=10")
---
We stopped in Evansville for the Building Trades Burger Cookout.
[](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972db/814495408/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180509.2&amount=10")
---
Parade season is officially upon us! Joe zig-zagged back and forth, shaking as many hands as possible at the 55th Annual Spring Blossom Parade in Nashville. (I was exhausted trying to keep up.)
[](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972db/814495408/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180509.2&amount=10")
---
Jason Kander, president of Let America Vote, joined us for church Sunday morning, a rally in Lake County after that, and a meet and greet in Lafayette to remind Hoosiers how important it is that they make their voices heard at the polls. We loved showing off the RV to him.
[](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972db/814495408/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180509.2&amount=10")
---
Joe ended the tour by greeting early voters on Monday in Indianapolis.
[](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972db/814495408/VEsHBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180509.2&amount=10")
---
The primary is now over, and we know we're going to face Rep. Braun in November. But honestly, that doesn't change much on our end. Joe's going to continue to work as hard as ever, going to all 92 counties, listening to constituents, and bringing a much-needed dose of Hoosier common sense to D.C.

**[Please make a contribution today so we can keep Joe's independent voice in the Senate.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972db/814495408/VEsHBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180509.2&amount=10")**

Thanks for reading!

Alana

P.S. Please make sure you're following us on **[Facebook](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972da/814495408/VEsHBw/ "https://www.facebook.com/JoeForIndiana/")** , **[Twitter](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972d5/814495408/VEsHBg/ "https://twitter.com/JoeforIndiana")** , and **[Instagram](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972d4/814495408/VEsHAQ/ "https://www.instagram.com/donnellyforindiana")**! I'll be continuing to take photos and posting updates. See you on the trail!

**[**CONTRIBUTE**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2169/7377f838/3f23b071/51f972db/814495408/VEsHAA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-donate?refcode=20180509.2&amount=10")**
---

|
---

Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
---

subject: Impeachment – The First Order of Business if Democrats Take Back the House
body:
Impeachment - The First Order of Business if Democrats Take Back the House No Images? [Click here](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-jydtghy-kujkulhluu-z/) [](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jydtghy-kujkulhluu-r/) [](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jydtghy-kujkulhluu-y/)
### [Impeachment - The First Order of Business if Democrats Take Back the House](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jydtghy-kujkulhluu-j/)
Over 70% of Democrats, according to recent polling, want President Trump impeached. And if Democrats take over the House of Representatives as a result of the election this November, you can be assured that the Democrat base will push Speaker Nancy Pelosi and her minions to do just that. I've blogged about it this week. Click below to read more. [Read this entry >>](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jydtghy-kujkulhluu-t/) _Previous Weekly Posts_ [](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jydtghy-kujkulhluu-i/)
### [The Left moves even further to the left](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jydtghy-kujkulhluu-d/)
Well The Left is at it again. Coming up with new ways to take more of your money, and give it to other people. A couple examples - from Democrats who want to be President are on the blog this week. Click below to read more. [Read this entry >>](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jydtghy-kujkulhluu-h/) [](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jydtghy-kujkulhluu-k/)
### [M](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jydtghy-kujkulhluu-u/)[y Brother Ron](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jydtghy-kujkulhluu-o/)
My brother, Ron, passed away last week. He was one of the best, and I will dearly miss him. So I've written my blog in tribute to him today. Click below to read more. [Read this entry >>](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jydtghy-kujkulhluu-b/) [](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jydtghy-kujkulhluu-n/)
### [Should You Have to Work for Your Food Stamps?](http://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jydtghy-kujkulhluu-p/)
There are over 40 million people in America who receive food stamps. We as taxpayers pay about $70 billion in taxes to provide those food stamps to recipients. So the question is, should you have to work to receive them? I'm on the blog today, talking about new legislation that would institute those requirements. Click below to read more. Peter -- You're invited to join us this [Saturday, May 12th](https://www.coreystewart.com/meet_and_greet_in_oldtown_20180512?e=ed823c4651e3363eb3aaad58e45e9481&utm_source=coreystewartusa&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=oldtown1&n=2), to hear why I'm asking for your **vote on June 12th** for the Republican nomination for Senate to defeat Tim Kaine. RSVP by clicking [here](https://www.coreystewart.com/meet_and_greet_in_oldtown_20180512?e=ed823c4651e3363eb3aaad58e45e9481&utm_source=coreystewartusa&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=oldtown1&n=3) for the Old Town Meet and Greet. _Here are a few statements I've heard on the trail---_ _**"If we're going to defeat Tim Kaine, we must win in Northern Virginia."**_ I am the only Republican to win countywide in Prince William since 2009, winning four times in the blue county. The only countywide Republican in all of Northern Virginia. No other candidate can say that. **"We must nominate someone with a proven conservative record."** I am the first in Virginia to put retired police officers in our schools, first to require local law enforcement to turn criminal illegal aliens over to ICE for deportation, and first to eliminate fee to obtain a concealed carry permit. ** _I have led Prince William to be the top county in Virginia for jobs. The overall tax bill is less than when I took office in 2006, 30% lower than any other Northern Virginia county._** **_"We must support President Trump and turn out his base."_**_I served as President Trump's Virginia State Chair in 2016 and have the support of Dr. Jerry Falwell, Laura Ingraham, and Steve Bannon because they know I'm the only candidate who will fight for President Trump's America First Agenda._ _**Meet and Greet in Old Town**

name: The White House
subject: A victory for the world
body:
Your 1600 Daily:
| | [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=06181f68dc278f3415d9be814d2997da50041e7a5c084841ecc51cdd9477e8f85ffbffad7d311cd66650ed11900924ae26168f7127bc81ed) The White House • May 10, 2018
## Earlier this morning
President Donald J. Trump welcomed home three American returnees from North Korea. “They are really three incredible people,” the President said from Joint Base Andrews. “And the fact that we were able to get them out so soon was really a tribute to a lot of things, including a certain process that's taking place right now.” In another major step, President Trump announced minutes ago that he will meet with North Korean Leader Kim Jong Un in Singapore on June 12. |
---
|

## A victory for the world
**At 2:26 a.m. ET, Secretary of State Mike Pompeo arrived at Joint Base Andrews,** bringing home three Americans who had been held in North Korea. Upon initial examination, the three appeared to be in good physical condition and were able to walk on the plane without assistance. President Trump was there to greet them.
**“This is a proud and memorable moment for America,”** Vice President Mike Pence said in a statement. “Our Secretary of State Mike Pompeo did a remarkable job overseeing the release of Kim Dong Chul, Tony Kim, and Kim Hak Song – and to them I say: safe travels and welcome home.”
**Video of the day:**[President Trump welcomes three U.S. returnees home. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=06181f68dc278f34b8f32c94c299fe1c66bff767188deeca8e34f9f7e5dc59f9dbb09881906d173f4b87cf3f73b25947ae7eaf9cec912561)

---
|

## What you need to know about the surge in border crossings
**Recently, there has been a surge in illegal border crossings.** From April 2017 to April 2018, the number of illegal border crossings more than tripled. April 2018 was also the second month in a row that saw more than 37,000 individuals apprehended after entering the country illegally along our Southwest border.
**Judicial rulings by activist courts make it virtually impossible** to remove even dangerous criminals. The result of these loopholes is that individuals can illegally enter the United States, make fraudulent asylum claims, and expect to be released into U.S. society while never appearing in court. This breakdown has contributed to a deportation backlog of more than 1 million cases.
**Congress must take real action** to secure our border and close the loopholes in our immigration system. In January, President Trump released [an immigration framework](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=06181f68dc278f34870041b50f7cf68c46894d5a71fd202c236d5e8b8674df0a0240d6615645b5f086466dd8a777e93ef1d39252404e9e96) that, if enacted into law, would have addressed many of these problems and secured our porous border.
[What you need to know about the recent surge in illegal border crossings. ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=06181f68dc278f3429962808fc3dc12c05c1c39e6fe2db1c31dcc7b073ad0bb9364ac87949aba49a97bc26d8af8f5bbbd4acd697689c759f)

---
|

## CIA nominee Gina Haspel gains momentum
**President Trump’s nominee for CIA Director** appeared before the Senate Intelligence Committee yesterday. Gina Haspel, a 33-year public servant at the Agency, spoke at length about her role in counterterrorism efforts following the September 11 attacks on America.
**A question from Sen. Marco Rubio (R-FL) summed up the hearing well:** “If someone like you, with your history, with your record of service and sacrifice and excellence . . . cannot be confirmed to head this agency, then who can?" The intelligence community agrees. A letter to lawmakers from 72 former CIA officers calls Haspel “the image of what we want a peer and leader to be.”
[President Trump’s nominee to lead the CIA continues to earn widespread support.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=06181f68dc278f34de5bd4a3b940a6c5dac05c1862fe69eb23244e367fc2ba172cf7a89be4423f374cfb305bc5bdd2de26a625cdc77cb589)

---
|

## Photo of the Day
Official White House Photo by D. Myles Cullen
**President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump welcome home three American returnees from North Korea** | May 10, 2018

name: Amanda Brown Lierman
subject: This is long, but I promise it's worth reading, Derek:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www
body:
By now you've probably heard about the "new DNC" -- and I'd like to make one thing clear: This is more than a branding exercise.
---
Derek --

By now you've probably heard about the "new DNC" -- and I'd like to make one thing clear: This is more than a branding exercise. It is a completely new organizing principle -- one designed to win elections -- that was launched by Tom Perez and Keith Ellison when they were elected to lead the DNC last year.

Our organization is quickly changing. We no longer live cycle-to-cycle, and there's no such thing as an "off year." For too long, the DNC has been largely focused on being involved in elections only every four years.

But the reality is that there is a chance to win an election almost every Tuesday from now through November -- whether it is a local school board contest, a city supervisor or mayor's race, a state legislature or governor's campaign, or a special election to fill an open seat in Congress. There are elections in every ZIP code and at all levels, and Democrats are competing everywhere, every year.

Basically, **"If it's Tuesday, it's Election Day."**

At the new DNC, we are targeting races and organizing communities in literally hundreds of municipalities, counties, and states across the country. And the key word here is "organizing." It is not a coincidence that my title is Political and Organizing Director, or that we are looking for staff with organizing backgrounds, or that we are currently hiring dozens of experienced organizers.

As we look to become more engaged locally, we're identifying individuals who can organize communities -- people who know how to knock on doors and make phone calls -- to create a stronger and more permanent infrastructure. Our commitment is to find people who are already plugged in at the local level, who will listen to local concerns, and who will foster long-term authentic relationships. We will then work with and through them to organize in those communities.

In order to implement this plan, we've come up with the following four-program approach to transform the Democratic Party state by state.

### EVERY ZIP CODE COUNTS
We are organizing and competing everywhere to win elections in 2018, 2019, and beyond -- 365 days a year. Our plan is an "Every ZIP Code Counts" strategy. To do that, we are engaging and supporting state parties -- providing them with additional resources to help them grow and to make sure they have the ability to hire experienced and talented staff.

Starting in October 2017 and through the Every ZIP Code Counts program, the DNC increased its monthly investment to $10,000 to every state party in the nation. This is a 33% increase over the base funding levels from 2016 and a 100% increase over 2015.

Recognizing that not all states are the same -- what Alabama needs may be quite different from what Arizona or Alaska needs -- my team has been on the ground in every state doing an analysis of each state party to determine what each state needs to win elections. We've met with party staff, partner organizations, and key allies to get a comprehensive understanding. And we're reviewing with the state parties what is currently working and what could be working better to help win local, statewide, and national elections.

We are partnering with our state parties to help create state-specific work plans with metrics, and we are making sure that states use their funding to strengthen their infrastructure and their overall effectiveness. The strategic plans include recommendations like hiring a communications director or requirements like voter file audits or cybersecurity precautions. They also include programmatic recommendations, such as strengthening candidate recruitment efforts or building community engagement capabilities.

In addition, we have launched a new program called the State Party Innovation Fund -- the first of its kind -- where competitive grants are awarded to state parties who put forth innovative ideas for engaging voters. These grants incentivize early organizing and supports efforts to engage African American, Latino, Asian American and Pacific Islander, millennial, and rural communities from now until Election Day.

### WINNING WITH PARTNERS
The DNC knows we can't win elections by doing all this work alone. Our party is part of a larger, multi-issue, progressive movement where we each have a role to play, and there are a number of organizations out there with different strengths and focuses. So, our greatest value-add as a partner is the role of convener.

To advance this strategy, DNC Deputy Chair Keith Ellison launched the Winning With Partners in 2018 program and has held a series of on-the-ground town hall meetings in states across the country. We are working with the newer groups, as well as working with traditional Democratic organizations that have been part of our progressive coalition for decades. Keith has already been to more than a dozen states and he has several more lined up this spring.

By pulling together state party leaders, community activists, and organizations new and old -- and listening to those grassroots leaders -- we discover common goals and agendas. Through this program, we are learning to share our resources to help one another win elections.

Winning With Partners in 2018 is part of our community engagement initiative and it is an example of the relationship building that is a priority of the new DNC. The idea is to really listen to community leaders and activists so that we are a partner in helping mobilize them before the November 2018 elections.

### THE IWILLVOTE CAMPAIGN
The DNC is committed to making every voice heard because we know when we get more people to vote, Democrats win! That's why we recently launched IWillVote.

We have an ambitious plan to reach 50 million Americans between now and Election Day to encourage them to vote for Democrats in 2018. The four components of this program will be: 1) getting our community to commit to vote, 2) ensuring they are registered to vote, 3) providing voter education and protection, and 4) running a get out the vote campaign.

Over the next six months, we hope that people across the country will visit IWillVote.com to learn how to register to vote, find out where their polling place is, and gather all the logistical information they need for Election Day. We will also be on the ground talking to voters throughout the year.

### BEST PRACTICES INSTITUTE
The new DNC will be a hub for learning and innovation where we creatively employ new technology, best practices, centralized training, and toolkits for state parties and individual candidates to use in every election.

We're not looking for cookie-cutter approaches, and we know each state is different. But sharing useful information and providing universal learnings can help all states to grow their base of support and become more effective.

The DNC is helping state parties and candidates with these trainings -- such as how to use digital tools for both fundraising and the development of stronger communities. Or which doors to knock on and which issues are motivating community activists. And increasingly, how local issues -- conversations about improving schools and personal safety -- can be just as compelling as the national issues we hear about every day.

Through the Best Practices Institute, we are investing in innovative solutions while focusing on the proven best practices that will help develop a permanent infrastructure that we will need to continue our progress in 2019, 2020, and beyond.

### MOVING FORWARD
Time is critical. With fewer than six months until the November midterm elections, we have a lot of work to do. Our success will come through the four programs outlined above: 1) Every ZIP Code Counts; 2) Winning With Partners in 2018; 3) the IWillVote campaign; and 4) the Best Practices Institute.

Thank you for your ongoing commitment to the Democratic Party. With your continued support, I am confident the DNC will help Democrats win up and down the ticket, reverse the dangerous policies of the Trump administration, and fight for the principles and values of our party.

All my best,

Amanda

Amanda Brown Lierman
Political and Organizing Director
Democratic National Committee

| | I know this is frustrating, for everyone. **But President Trump is, once again, thinking about cutting Title X funding from family planning clinics, including Planned Parenthood.** Which means we must, once again, rise up to stop him. _To deny women access to critical health services is to deny their rights._**_[Add your name to make 100% clear to President Trump that you support women's access to care, you support Planned Parenthood, and you won't let him get away with denying women's right to care.](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fc7/622b7b5b/1a8d00c0/307b8626/2460131848/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180507") _** This new push isn't just about denying Planned Parenthood funds -- it's aimed at denying Title X funds to any clinic that provides, recommends, or refers women to abortion services. **This is, in effect, a domestic gag rule.** Trump wants to pre-determine what reproductive services women can get. This goes without saying -- but Trump is not a doctor. He's not qualified to tell women what care they can and cannot receive. We need to let him know that we will not stand by and let this happen. [**_Add your name >>_**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fc7/622b7b5b/1a8d00c0/307b8626/2460131848/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180507") Thank you. Debbie Wasserman Schultz

| Today, we welcome President Donald Trump and Vice President Mike Pence back to Indiana. Our great state is a place where President Trump has made history before -- securing the 2016 Republican nomination, picking a vice president, and announcing major policy initiatives.

**And today, he'll write history again, making a joint****appearance**** with Vice President Pence to stand shoulder-to-shoulder with Indiana's Republican nominee for U.S. Senate AND our next United States Senator, **[Mike Braun](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=31188da4ea&e=1acd9bb3d6)**.**

President Trump and Mike Braun have a lot in common. They're both conservative outsiders -- having built their track records in the private sector, not government. President Trump is shaking things up in Washington, and Mike Braun will be there as his partner in the Senate.

[Learn more about Mike Braun](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=69cc669879&e=1acd9bb3d6), and how he'll provide Hoosiers with a consistent, conservative voice in Washington!

Hear directly from Marc Lotter and Marty Obst, two Hoosiers who hold key roles within the Trump-Pence administration. They'll provide an inside look into the 2016 campaign, how Governor Mike Pence became Vice President Mike Pence, and how President Trump is driving a conservative agenda in Washington!

Learn more about the dinner, and claim your seat [here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=205b21fe03&e=1acd9bb3d6).

And today, he'll write history again, making a jointappearance with Vice President Pence to stand shoulder-to-shoulder with Indiana's Republican nominee for U.S. Senate AND our next United States Senator, . President Trump and Mike Braun have a lot in common. They're both conservative outsiders -- having built their track records in the private sector, not government. President Trump is shaking things up in Washington, and Mike Braun will be there as his partner in the Senate. , and how he'll provide Hoosiers with a consistent, conservative voice in Washington! | "LEARN ABOUT MIKE BRAUN" | | | You've voted. And now it's time to stay in the action by attending our 2018 Indiana Republican Spring Dinner! Hear directly from Marc Lotter and Marty Obst, two Hoosiers who hold key roles within the Trump-Pence administration. They'll provide an inside look into the 2016 campaign, how Governor Mike Pence became Vice President Mike Pence, and how President Trump is driving a conservative agenda in Washington! Learn more about the dinner, and claim your seat . | "RESERVE YOUR SEAT NOW!" | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2018 Indiana Republican State Committee, Inc., All rights reserved.

| Friend, I wanted to update you on the status of my re-election campaign after the primary election earlier this week. **On Tuesday night, Nancy Pelosi's handpicked candidate and major donor, Kathy Manning, was chosen in the Democrat Primary to run against me.** Manning was able to win that primary by using the same strategy she will be using against me in November - by trying to buy this race for her liberal-insider agenda. **Kathy Manning has given nearly a million dollars to the who’s who of liberal elites - Nancy Pelosi, John Edwards, Hillary Clinton, just to name a few.** Now it's payback time for the Democrat Establishment. Everyone in Washington knows that Kathy Manning will be a reliable vote for Nancy Pelosi and that's why the DCCC anointed her as their pick late last year. **We already know what their agenda will be: raise our taxes (Nancy Pelosi admitted that this week), impeach our President, and push their liberal ideology of bigger government on us.** All you have to do is look at who’s supporting Kathy Manning’s campaign - groups like Emily’s List and a number of America’s largest pro-abortion groups and most recently, San Francisco billionaire Tom Steyer whose singular mission is impeaching President Trump. We are less than 6 months from Election Day in November and we can't let them succeed. I am so thankful for all of your support this far and I hope you will consider standing shoulder to shoulder with me in this fight over the next 6 months. [Will you help me send a message this week - the same week Nancy Pelosi bought her donor Kathy Manning the Democrat nomination - that we are not sending her a rubber stamp?](http://click.mlsend2.com/link/c/YT04ODg0Nzg0OTAxNTg5NjI2NjImYz1xMXczJmU9MTkyOCZiPTE3NDkwNTYwMCZkPWc4ejh4NWQ=.dbVZIm-omq6oWx4gzQIDohEa16ptHqpBOkeWmzUYFbQ) My message hasn't changed and I will continue fighting hard for lower taxes, less power in Washington, safer communities, and more freedom for you and your family. Friends --

I'm the son of a construction worker and a bus driver, and I'm running for Governor because I believe every single person -- no matter where they're born, or what their parents do -- should have the opportunity to thrive in this state. Hard work is a virtue.

With 100 days to go, this campaign is heating up. And I want to remind you why I'm running for Governor, and why it's time for Florida to have fresh, progressive leadership for people across the state.

**[Watch my latest ad on how we can rebuild Florida today.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb30a1/9e00690/44da99b1/24021c99/3030573368/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-ad-may18?gpsid=13_55_437&refcode=em18_180510_MayAd_fnd_gq_act_andg_AG&utm_campaign=em18_180510_MayAd_fnd_gq_act_andg_AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=agil&utm_content=2+-+Watch+my+latest+ad+on+how+we+can+rebuild")**

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Save the open internet.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Add your name: Protect net neutrality for the nation. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd795e6/2f5ea88d/719371b0/2cbcda84/810877009/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/protect-net-neutrality?utm_source=ema_2018-05-10_nn&source=ema_2018-05-10_nn&utm_campaign=nn&utm_medium=ema") Friend, We are so close. Yesterday, a group of 50 pro-internet senators in D.C. moved to force a vote in the next week to save net neutrality. 50 senators -- which means we're one vote away from protecting a free and open internet in the Senate. We don't want two worlds: one for people who can afford fast internet, one for all of those who will be stuck in the slow lane. One for big companies who can pay for premium channels, one for all the independent media and small businesses who can't compete. That's why we're leading in the states. With the leadership of governors and state legislatures, states like Washington are taking action and passing net neutrality for our residents. Together, we're showing what leadership to demand a free and open internet looks like, but the work isn't done. **It goes without saying, the internet crosses state lines. We need a national solution for what should be a right for all of us: a free and open internet.** **The FCC made a terrible decision in December to end net neutrality. We have a chance to put it back on the books.[And that's where you come in: can you add your name to our petition for Congress to show how important net neutrality is to us?](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd795e6/2f5ea88d/719371b0/2cbcda84/810877009/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/protect-net-neutrality?utm_source=ema_2018-05-10_nn&source=ema_2018-05-10_nn&utm_campaign=nn&utm_medium=ema")** The Senate is demanding a vote within the next week on a Congressional Review Act, which has the power to nullify or restore regulations affected by federal government agencies like the FCC. Going through Congress is our last chance to pass a law that will save net neutrality nationwide. Under my leadership, Washington was the first state to pass net neutrality protections this year. We've made good progress here at home, but Washington businesses will still be affected if federal law permits an internet service provider free-for-all with the rest of the country. [**So take a minute out of your day to save the open internet. Add your name now to tell Congress to vote for net neutrality.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd795e6/2f5ea88d/719371b0/2cbcda84/810877009/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/protect-net-neutrality?utm_source=ema_2018-05-10_nn&source=ema_2018-05-10_nn&utm_campaign=nn&utm_medium=ema") This is our last chance. We've got to act now. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [ADD YOUR NAME »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd795e6/2f5ea88d/719371b0/2cbcda84/810877009/VEsC/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/protect-net-neutrality?utm_source=ema_2018-05-10_nn&source=ema_2018-05-10_nn&utm_campaign=nn&utm_medium=ema")
